clues.clue: This term, often associated with a one-armed contraption in a casino, is also used to refer to slot machines.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which of these terms is often used to refer to slot machines?' with answer 'One-armed bandit' — a colloquial general-knowledge MISC fact.

clues.clue: According to Sherlock Holmes, 'If you eliminate the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the...' this.

clues.clue: When visiting Finland, tradition holds that Santa leaves his sleigh behind and instead rides on this animal.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'When visiting Finland, Santa leaves his sleigh behind and rides on what?' — obscure cultural/holiday MISC trivia; answer 'A goat'.

clues.clue: The word 'Honolulu,' the capital of Hawaii, translates to this in English.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'What is the meaning of Honolulu?' with answer 'Sheltered harbor' — etymology/place-name MISC trivia not fitting neatly into GEOGRAPHY or LANGUAGE as a pure category.

finals.title: FAMOUS EXPRESSIONS
finals.clue: This familiar two-word expression describes a diversionary tactic used to draw attention away from the main issue, often attributed to the practice of dragging smoked fish across a trail to mislead hounds.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'What is the familiar expression for something that draws attention away from the main issue?' — a common-expression MISC fact. Answer is 'Red Herring'.

finals.title: CURRENCY OF THE WORLD
finals.clue: This South Asian nation's official currency is called the Rupee, used for everyday transactions from Mumbai to Chennai.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'What is the currency of India' and source answer is 'Rupee'; the clue reverses this to ask for the country, fitting a MISC/currency theme appropriate for a final clue.

finals.title: WORLD RECORDS & FIRSTS
finals.clue: This South American country contains the Pantanal, recognized as the largest tropical wetland area on the entire planet, spanning over 150,000 square kilometers.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which country is home to the Pantanal, the largest tropical wetland area in the world?' with answer 'Brazil' — a hard geography fact about a world record superlative, suitable as a Final clue.

finals.title: SWORN TO HEAL
finals.clue: This oath, which originated in ancient Greece and is still associated with the medical profession today, is traditionally taken by doctors upon entering practice.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'what is the name of the oath traditionally taken by doctors, which originated in ancient Greece and is still associated with the medical profession today'; answer is 'The Hippocratic Oath', a cultural/societal institution fitting MISC topic.
